issues.title: Stored XSS risk from unescaped JSON-LD in destination detail page
issues.rationale: `name`, `description`, and `image` come from database content. Injecting `JSON.stringify(jsonLd)` directly into a `<script>` tag via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` does not escape `</script>` and related characters, so malicious content in destination fields can terminate the script block and execute attacker-controlled HTML/JS.
issues.recommendation: Serialize JSON-LD with HTML-safe escaping before injection (at minimum escape `<`, `>`, `&`, U+2028, U+2029), or use a trusted helper that guarantees script-safe JSON serialization. Keep lint guardrails enabled instead of bypassing `noDangerouslySetInnerHtml` for DB-backed data.

issues.title: Stored XSS risk from unescaped JSON-LD in destinations list page
issues.rationale: This page also embeds DB-derived fields (`nameKo/nameEn`, `slug`, `imageUrl`) into a raw JSON-LD `<script>` via `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`. If any stored value contains script-closing payloads, it can break out of the JSON-LD block and run arbitrary script in visitors’ browsers.
issues.recommendation: Apply the same script-safe JSON serialization fix here as well. Prefer a shared utility for JSON-LD emission that always escapes dangerous characters before rendering.
